name: Torrijas - circular route on the Cholesterol Route
name_original: Torrijas – circular por la Ruta del Colesterol
region_intro: Hiking around Torrijas offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Javalambre, situated at 1,360 meters above sea level. The region features mountainous terrain with notable peaks and unique vegetation patterns. Trails often traverse varied environments, including areas where the Riodeva river originates, characterized by ravines and waterfalls. This area provides a range of hiking experiences across different elevations and natural settings. Best hiking trails around Torrijas The most popular hiking route is Torrijas - circular route on the Cholesterol Route, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 43 minutes to complete. This easy path is ideal for a leisurely walk around the town. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico del Buitre Circular Trail, a difficult 12.5 miles (20.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain, offering a substantial challenge. Local hikers also love the Javalambre – circular from Javalambre Station via Rabadá y Navarro Refuge, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Javalambre landscape, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Hiking around Torrijas is defined by mountainous terrain, river ravines, and unique "leopard skin" vegetation on the massif of Pico Javalambre. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging ascents. The routes in Torrijas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 91 hikers have used komoot to explore Torrijas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Torrijas?
A: Torrijas, nestled in the Sierra de Javalambre, offers a wide variety of hiking trails suitable for all fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy, leisurely strolls to challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The landscape includes mountainous terrain, river ravines, and unique vegetation patterns.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in the area?
A: Yes, for an easy and pleasant walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981474858/torrijas-circular-route-on-the-cholesterol-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijas - circular route on the Cholesterol Route&lt;/a&gt;. This 6.2 km circular path is ideal for families and can be completed in under two hours, offering a gentle introduction to the local scenery.

Q: Where can I find trails with waterfalls or unique natural features?
A: The region is home to &lt;strong&gt;The Amanaderos of Riodeva&lt;/strong&gt;, where the Riodeva river originates. This area features a path along the river ravine with steps, footbridges, and several waterfalls, depending on the river's flow. You can explore this natural monument via &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Amanaderos of Riodeva&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976345844/pico-del-buitre-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Buitre Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option. This difficult 20.1 km route features substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: The Sierra de Javalambre offers panoramic views, especially from peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the unique 'leopard skin' vegetation effect on the massif of Pico Javalambre after the snow melts. Another significant landmark is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2305519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory (Pico del Buitre)&lt;/a&gt;, which can be reached via some of the longer trails.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Torrijas area?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. Besides the easy 'Cholesterol Route,' a moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976332845/javalambre-circular-from-javalambre-station-via-rabada-y-navarro-refuge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javalambre – circular from Javalambre Station via Rabadá y Navarro Refuge&lt;/a&gt;. This 9.2 km trail takes you through the stunning Sierra de Javalambre landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking around Torrijas?
A: The region is generally pleasant for hiking outside of the peak winter months when snow can cover higher elevations. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. The 'leopard skin' vegetation effect is best seen after the snow has melted.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Torrijas?
A: The hiking trails around Torrijas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrain to river ravines, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any shelters or refuges along the trails?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;Collado del Buey Shelter&lt;/strong&gt; is located at 1,720 meters altitude on the Sierra de Javalambre. This restored refugio offers a basic but charming stop for hikers, providing a place to rest during longer excursions.

Q: Can I find any moderate difficulty hikes that offer good views?
A: Absolutely. The 'View of Las Dos Villas – Torrijas Loop' is a moderate 10.9 km loop that provides excellent views of the surrounding area and requires a moderate fitness level. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hotel Balneario El Paraíso loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 8 km long.

Q: Are there any short, easy walks with interesting features nearby?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31870670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcos de las Salinas – Baños Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.1 km hike. This route offers a pleasant stroll and includes the Baños Waterfall, making it a nice option for a shorter outing.
tips: In the middle of the Sierra de Javalambre at 1360 meters above sea level is this picturesque town typical of the interior of Teruel. It has a valuable historical, cultural and gastronomic heritage that you have to discover in addition to offering several hiking tours in the area.
